description:
You are acting as a database reliability engineer who wants to keep backup‐related configuration outside the shell history.  
Your goal is to place the required settings in a dotenv file and then capture those exported values in a plain-text log so that an auditing robot can later verify exactly what was loaded into the environment.

Target directory  
/home/user/db_backup

Required steps (do NOT list commands, just carry them out):

1. Ensure the directory above exists.  
2. Inside that directory, create a file named “.env” that contains **exactly** the following five lines (no trailing spaces, one newline at the end of the file):  
   DB_HOST=localhost  
   DB_PORT=5432  
   DB_USER=backup_user  
   DB_PASS=s3cr3tP@ss  
   BACKUP_DIR=/var/backups/postgres  

3. Load those variables into your current shell environment from “.env” and immediately create a second file, “env_capture.log”, in the same directory.  
   • “env_capture.log” must contain exactly five lines, listed in **alphabetical order by key**, each in KEY=VALUE format, followed by a single trailing newline.  
   • The alphabetical ordering is: BACKUP_DIR, DB_HOST, DB_PASS, DB_PORT, DB_USER.  
   • Example of the required final appearance (values must match those in .env):  
     BACKUP_DIR=/var/backups/postgres  
     DB_HOST=localhost  
     DB_PASS=s3cr3tP@ss  
     DB_PORT=5432  
     DB_USER=backup_user  

Successfully completing the task means both “/home/user/db_backup/.env” and “/home/user/db_backup/env_capture.log” exist with the exact contents and ordering described above. Automated tests will read both files verbatim; any deviation will be marked as a failure.  
The entire job can be finished with roughly two to three shell commands; use any approach you like as long as the end state matches the specification.
truth:
Expected files and contents after the agent finishes:

1. Path: /home/user/db_backup/.env  
   Exact bytes (including final newline):  
   "DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=backup_user
DB_PASS=s3cr3tP@ss
BACKUP_DIR=/var/backups/postgres
"

2. Path: /home/user/db_backup/env_capture.log  
   Exact bytes (including final newline):  
   "BACKUP_DIR=/var/backups/postgres
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PASS=s3cr3tP@ss
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=backup_user
"
